articles.title: John Healey wanted UK to join global investment bank to raise defence funds - BBC

articles.title: Switzerland is voting on whether to cap its population at 10 million. Here's what to know - CNBC
articles.description: Switzerland votes on a population cap that could tighten immigration and put its EU free-movement agreement under pressure.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV's flight home from Spain was grounded so the king came to his aid - NPR
articles.description: Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

articles.title: Anthropic disables Fable and Mythos AI models after U.S. government bars it from giving foreigners access - Fortune
articles.description: The directive would even bar Anthropic's own foreign employees from using Fable and Mythos. Anthropic called the government position "a misunderstanding".

articles.title: Gene Shalit, ‘Today’ movie critic and purveyor of puns, dies at 100 - The Washington Post
articles.description: He appeared on the morning show for four decades before retiring in 2010.

articles.title: Tent collapses during Virginia church's 20th anniversary celebration, killing 1 and injuring 22 - AP News
articles.description: Officials say a large tent collapsed during a Virginia church’s 20th anniversary celebration, killing one person and injuring nearly two dozen others. A spokesperson for Bedford County says heavy rain, lightning and strong winds tore through Moneta on Friday …

articles.title: Folarin Balogun scores twice as USMNT beats Paraguay 4-1 in World Cup opener - Yahoo Sports
articles.description: The U.S. men's national team kicked off its World Cup campaign with a thrilling win over Paraguay on Friday night in Los Angeles.

articles.title: Live Updates: "Final, agreed upon text" of U.S.-Iran peace deal has been reached, Pakistan says - CBS News
articles.description: Iran's foreign minister said a deal has "never been closer" and that the details of a memorandum of understanding with the U.S. will be shared publicly "in due course."

articles.title: Courts deny last-ditch Kennedy Center bid to delay removing Trump’s name - The Washington Post
articles.description: After courts knocked down attempts to delay the signage change, crews built scaffolding but failed to remove President Donald Trump’s name by midnight.

articles.title: Justice Dept. approves Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery - NPR
articles.description: The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN.

articles.title: A Closer Look at Mary-Kate and Ashley's Hair Evolution - WWD

articles.title: Judge rules Trump can stage UFC fights on the White House's South Lawn this weekend - PBS
articles.description: A federal judge ruled on Friday that the White House is allowed to stage a UFC show this weekend in an elaborate ring already built on the South Lawn to celebrate the nation’s 250th anniversary — on President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday.
